title: Screen Supervisor
description: A walkthrough of the Screen source and its supervisor-style persona behaviors.

steps.title: Open Vision -> Screen
steps.body: This tutorial focuses on the Screen source. If you do not see a Screen tab, first include Screen in the Vision source selector on the Core page.

steps.title: Source Tab
steps.body: The Screen source provides ambient desktop context. It is useful for knowing what the user is currently doing, reading, watching, or editing without turning every screen capture into a visible user request.

steps.title: Supervisor Tab
steps.body: Now switch to the Supervisor child tab. This is where persona-driven reactions are defined for the Screen source.

steps.title: Persona Ownership
steps.body: Each Screen Supervisor persona owns a list of behaviors. The active persona determines tone, while each behavior defines what on-screen pattern matters and how NC should react.

steps.title: Behavior Cards
steps.body: Each behavior card follows the same structure: enabled state, Visual Trigger, Action, and optional Advanced controls. Advanced now includes strictness, emotion override, and an Allow recurring commentary toggle for behaviors that should keep bantering instead of firing only once. Newly added behavior cards start disabled so blank cards do not go live immediately.

steps.title: Best Use
steps.body: Screen Supervisor is best for ambient patterns such as working in a certain app, browsing a certain kind of content, or visibly composing something that deserves a character reaction.
